recalls.Description: Igloo has expanded its recall to include an additional 130,000 units of the 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers manufactured before January 2024. The manufacturing date is marked on the bottom of the cooler, indicated by a circular pattern with an arrow pointing to the month and the last two digits of the year. The recalled coolers feature a tow handle and display the "IGLOO" brand name on the side. Various color combinations are available for these coolers, and the model numbers can be found on labels on the bottom of each unit. The specific models affected include the LATITUDE 90 QT ROLLER and MAXCOLD LATITUDE 90 QT ROLLER produced between July 2020 and February 2023.
recalls.Title: Igloo Expands Recall of 90 Qt. Rolling Coolers Due to Amputation and Crushing Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Igloo at 888-943-5182 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, email igloo90qt@sedgwick.com, or visit www.igloo90qtrecall.expertinquiry.com or http://www.igloocoolers.com and select "Recall Information" for further details.

Products.Name: Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 130,000 additional coolers (In addition, about 20,000 in Canada and 5,900 in Mexico) (More than one million Igloo coolers were previously recalled in February 2025)

Injuries.Name: Since the recall was announced in February 2025, Igloo has received 78 reports of fingertip injuries, including 26 resulting in fingertip amputations, bone fractures or lacerations.

Manufacturers.Name: Igloo Products Corp., of Katy, Texas

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco, Target, Academy, Dick's Sporting Goods, and various other retailers across the country, as well as online at Amazon.com and Igloocoolers.com from January 2019 through January 2025, priced between $80 and $140.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fingertip amputation and crushing due to tow handle pinch.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the coolers immediately and contact Igloo for a complimentary replacement handle.

recalls.metaDescription: Igloo recalls 90 Qt. Rolling Coolers due to serious fingertip hazards. Stop using and contact for a free replacement handle.

recalls.Description:
Igloo is recalling its 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers manufactured before January 2024 due to safety hazards. The coolers feature a tow handle and have 'IGLOO' printed on the side. Consumers can identify affected models by the date codes imprinted on the bottom, which indicate the month and year of manufacture. Several models with production dates ranging from 2019 to 2023 are included in this recall.

The tow handle poses a risk of pinching fingers, leading to potential fingertip amputation and crushing injuries. Consumers are urged to stop using these coolers immediately and reach out to Igloo for a free replacement handle to ensure their safety.
recalls.Title: Igloo Recalls Over One Million 90 Quart Rolling Coolers Due to Amputation and Crushing Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Igloo at 888-943-5182 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email igloo90qt@sedgwick.com, or visit www.igloo90qtrecall.expertinquiry.com or http://www.igloocoolers.com and select 'Recall Information' for further details.

Products.Name: Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,060,000 (In addition, about 47,000 in Canada and 23,000 in Mexico)

Remedies.Name: Stop using the coolers and contact Igloo for a free replacement handle.

recalls.metaDescription: Igloo recalls over one million 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers due to fingertip amputation risks. Contact Igloo for a free replacement handle.

recalls.Description: Anker is recalling its EverFrost battery-powered coolers due to a potential fire hazard linked to the lithium-ion battery packs. These coolers, designed for outdoor activities like camping and fishing, feature a dark gray exterior with wheels and a drop-down handle. The blue charging port allows for alternative power sources such as plug-in or solar charging. The product name 'Anker EverFrost' is displayed in silver and blue on the cooler's side, with the model number located on the bottom surface. The affected models include the Anker Detachable Battery Pack for Powered Cooler (A17B0) and EverFrost Powered Coolers in various sizes (A17A0, A17A1, A17A2).
recalls.Title: Recall of Anker EverFrost Battery-Powered Coolers Due to Fire Risk from Lithium-Ion Batteries
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled coolers immediately and contact Anker at 800-988-7973 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email support@anker.com, or visit https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus. A LiveChat option is available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us, and further details can be found at https://www.anker.com/anker-solix/cooler-battery-recall-pps.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of overheating, smoke and/or fire. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Anker Innovations Limited, of Hong Kong

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, TD Synnex, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at kickstarter.com from May 2023 through September 2023, priced between $250 and $950.

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion batteries posing a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Stop using the coolers and request a free replacement lithium-ion battery pack from Anker. Dispose of recalled batteries according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Anker recalls EverFrost coolers due to fire risks from lithium-ion batteries. Immediate action recommended for consumers.
